items.question_text: Complete: Special VFR operations are authorized within the lateral boundaries of Class B, Class C, Class D, or Class E surface areas when weather conditions are below _____ weather minimums.
items.explanation: SVFR is specifically for operations when conditions are below basic VFR minimums.

items.question_text: Under 3-12-2, what is the primary separation requirement for SVFR departure operations?
items.explanation: SVFR departures require separation from other SVFR and IFR aircraft. The controller must ensure aircraft are separated before issuing the SVFR clearance.

choices.text: No separation is required because SVFR aircraft provide their own separation.
Separation must be ensured between SVFR aircraft and other SVFR and IFR traffic.
Radar separation of at least 10 miles with the SVFR aircraft.
Separation only from IFR aircraft, but not from other SVFR aircraft.

items.question_text: Under 3-12-3, what is the arrival separation requirement for SVFR operations?
items.explanation: SVFR arrivals must be separated from other SVFR and IFR arrivals. The same separation philosophy applies as with departures — SVFR aircraft do not provide their own separation from IFR traffic.

choices.text: SVFR arrivals are sequenced only behind IFR aircraft, not ahead of them.
Separation must be ensured between SVFR arrivals and other SVFR and IFR traffic.
SVFR arrivals have priority and require no separation from other aircraft.
Only vertical separation is required — lateral and longitudinal separation are not applicable.
